summary:
Funded Trading Plus (FTP) scores 77/100 overall after testing, driven by its wide range of challenge formats, including one step, two step, and instant funding models. Account sizes run from $5,000 to $200,000, with profit splits starting at 80% across standard programs but can be upgraded to 90%.

FTP also is known for its aggressive scaling potential where you can grow accounts up to $5 million if performance criteria are met. Flexible payout cycles (every 3, 5, or 7 days) and optional profit split upgrades add further customisation, but the score is held back mainly by limited transparency around spreads, which makes it harder to fully assess trading costs in advance, but overall FTP remains a strong option if you want variety, scaling upside, and flexible evaluation structures.
pros: Profit split from 80% to 90% with upgrades
Aggressive scaling plan up to $5 million
Six distinct challenge types for different trading styles
Five trading platforms including MT4, MT5, cTrader, Match-Trader, DXTrade
cons: Minimal transparency on spreads and trading costs
Higher fees for instant funding accounts
DXTrade has reported lag and connectivity issues
Customer support only available in English
bestFor: Experienced traders who want diverse challenge formats, aggressive scaling potential up to $5 million, and flexible payout cycles with optional profit split upgrades
notIdealFor: Traders who need clear spread data upfront, beginners seeking comprehensive educational resources, or those who prefer a single streamlined evaluation path

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On Funded Trading Plus
verdictBlocks.body:
Funded Trading Plus provides a solid option for traders seeking access to capital through a clear evaluation process. With a strong community, a competitive 80% profit split, and flexible account types, the firm offers a viable path for traders looking to scale their operations.

However, the lack of transparency on trading costs and issues with the DXTrade platform may be concerns for some traders. Overall, Funded Trading Plus is a strong contender in the prop trading space, particularly for those who value community support and straightforward challenges.

faq.a: Funded Trading Plus is generally considered a legitimate prop firm with a large and active trading community and a long operating history compared to many newer firms. It offers clearly defined challenge structures, documented rules, and a transparent account dashboard, which helps traders understand how evaluations and funded accounts operate. That said, FTP is not a broker-backed prop firm and some traders raise concerns around spread transparency and platform consistency.

faq.q: Does Funded Trading Plus offer a scaling plan?
faq.a: Yes. Funded Trading Plus offers an aggressive performance-based scaling plan, allowing you to grow your account up to $5 million if profit milestones are met. Scaling is optional and based on hitting predefined profit targets on your funded account. There are no time-based requirements, but higher account sizes and payout conditions depend on the specific program you choose and continued rule compliance.

Yes. Funded Trading Plus does pay out profits, provided you follow the trading rules. Payouts are not fixed weekly. Instead, you can choose payout cycles of 3 days, 5 days, or 7 days, depending on the account type and payout option selected. Withdrawals are available via bank transfer or cryptocurrency, with a minimum payout amount of $50.

faq.q: What are the profit split and fees for Funded Trading Plus?
faq.a: Profit splits start at 80% by default but depending on the program you select and any optional upgrades at checkout, you can increase your profit split to 85% or 90%. Higher payout percentages are not automatic and are tied to specific models or add-ons. Challenge fees vary by program, your one step challenges start from $119, while instant funding accounts cost more depending on account size. All FTP challenges and instant accounts offer sizes ranging from $5,000 to $200,000.

faq.q: Which trading platforms does Funded Trading Plus offer?
faq.a: Funded Trading Plus offers Match-Trader, DXTrade, MetaTrader 4 (MT4), MetaTrader 5 (MT5), and cTrader. MT4 and MT5 are well suited if you use Expert Advisors or custom indicators. Match-Trader offers TradingView integration and works well for discretionary trading. cTrader provides advanced tools but carries a $25 platform fee. DXTrade is available, though it is generally less popular due to performance limitations.

faq.q: Is Funded Trading Plus a scam?
faq.a: No. Funded Trading Plus is not a scam. It is a legitimate prop firm that provides funded trading opportunities through structured evaluation and instant funding models. That said, you should fully understand the rules, drawdown calculations, and payout conditions before signing up. FTP is best suited if you are comfortable trading within strict risk frameworks and prop firm rules.
